summary: Retract the ten seam root exports to nothing ‚Äî the payload is internal by ruling, and the root face must stop naming its rows, its compiler, and its version

edit.description: Retract all ten. Off `./declarations.js` (84-94): the type block `AssemblyFactRow`/`ClauseRow`/`Declarations`/`EdgePlacements`/`KindFactRow`/`RequirementRow`/`SatisfiesRow`, and the value line `SEAM_VERSION`/`compileDeclarations`. Off `./emit.js` (97): `PayloadMember` out of the `export type` list ‚Äî `EmitResult`/`RegistrationFact`/`ResolveOptions`/`SettingsResidue` and `emit` stay. Delete the 'Declaration rows ‚Äî the erased program the seam carries' section comment (83) with its block; no subpath home, no re-export elsewhere. The module header (1-14) narrates `emit` compiling to the JSON pipe and stays true ‚Äî leave it unless the retraction makes a sentence false. `declarations.ts` keeps every symbol exported module-side (its own 42-48 block, `EdgePlacements` 485, `compileDeclarations` 719, `SEAM_VERSION` 743): this retracts the ROOT face only, not the internal one.

Two sites, not one ‚Äî the inbox note named only the second. The value import off `../src/index.js` carries `compileDeclarations` (12), and the type import (25) carries `ClauseRow`/`RequirementRow` alongside `Predicate`/`Requirement`, which are root nouns and stay. Retarget the retracted names to `../src/declarations.js`, keeping `clause`/`count`/`harness`/‚Ä¶ and `Predicate`/`Requirement` on the root ‚Äî the SDK's own suite reaching past the public face is the honest spelling of a spec-internal seam, not a workaround.

Third retarget site: `compileDeclarations` in the root value import (22, off `../src/index.js` at 35) moves to `../src/declarations.js`; the ~20 bare call sites are unaffected. Then extend the existing removed-exports test (623-628) ‚Äî it already walks `import * as sdk` (36) for names the root must not carry (`decision`/`law`/`bound`/`genre`/`genreValue`) ‚Äî with the retraction's two value names, so the value half is pinned where a re-export cannot silently restore it. Extend that test; never add a second one beside it. The eight type-only names erase at runtime and are unreachable from that walk ‚Äî `root_exports.test.ts`'s `.d.ts` walk plus strict `tsc` is what holds them.

The closure walk's generated-family exclusion, re-pointed at the ruled boundary. Its header (10-14) justifies the exclusion by the OPEN `(seam-rows-public-face)` fork and names `Declarations`/`PayloadMember` as root-exported aliases ‚Äî both claims retire with this entry: the fork is ruled, and neither name is root-exported after it. Re-word to the ruling: the row family is internal, versioned in lockstep, and reaches the face only by inference through `EmitResult` ‚Äî nameability was never the boundary, the never-made stability promise is. The `continue` skipping generated-declared exports (88) goes dead the same moment ‚Äî no root export is declared under `generated/` once `Declarations`/`PayloadMember` retract; delete it rather than leave a guard for a case that cannot arise. `isAuthoringNoun`'s own `generated/` filter (77) stays live and load-bearing: `EmitResult` still NAMES both types (emit.ts:653/655), and that filter is what keeps the walk from calling them holes.

tests.asserts: the root namespace carries neither `compileDeclarations` nor `SEAM_VERSION` ‚Äî the value half of the retraction pinned against a silent re-export
the closure still holds over the retracted face: every type a root-exported declaration names is root-exported, the generated row family excluded by declaration file

acceptance: The root face names no seam row type, no `EdgePlacements`, no `SEAM_VERSION`, no `compileDeclarations`, no `PayloadMember`; the rows reach a caller only by inference through `emit()`'s return (pipeline.md, "Emit": the payload is internal, versioned in lockstep, not a designed public interchange; one is admitted when a consumer exists, and none does). `pnpm --dir sdk test` green (strict `tsc` + `node --test`); `cargo test` green.
notes: Ruled 2026-07-16; scoped at 0c25b2c. Re-verified on disk: zero consumers outside sdk/test ‚Äî `.temper/harness.ts` imports `emit`/`harness`, both READMEs the same, `docs/proposals/` none, no Rust fixture spells a retracted name; the engine mirrors `SEAM_VERSION` independently (src/drift.rs:512) and is untouched. The inbox undercounted the retarget: three sites, not one.

summary: `MAX_IMPORT_HOPS = 5` cites a memory doc that now reads four hops: the gate constant and its own source disagree, and the doc sentence cannot settle which counting is temper's

gate.reason: A human probe rules the hop semantics ‚Äî the doc text cannot. Fetched live at 420da04 (code.claude.com/docs/en/memory, retrieved 2026-07-16): 'Imported files can recursively import other files, with a maximum depth of four hops.' Both readings survive that sentence: the root's own import is hop 1 and the chain caps at four edges (‚áí 4), or 'imported files ... recursively import' names a recursion four deep ATOP that first import (‚áí 5, today's constant). Only measuring the runtime's actual load depth decides it, the headless-testbed act that produced the `(mention-gate-containment)` evidence ‚Äî never plan's to guess and never build's to fetch, because guessing here edits a live gate's semantics: a cap too low forges a dead-member finding on a member that loads, a cap too high suppresses a real one (intent.md invariant 2).

edit.description: The constant and the cite that justifies it. `MAX_IMPORT_HOPS = 5` (62) sits under a doc (58-62) asserting 'the `at-import` grammar is recursion-capped at five hops (code.claude.com/docs/en/memory, retrieved 2026-07-02)' ‚Äî a number the live page does not say. temper's own indexing IS decidable on disk and needs no ruling: `live_members` (486-527) seeds every own-live member and runs `for _ in 0..MAX_IMPORT_HOPS` BFS rounds (513), one import edge per round, so the constant counts EDGES traversed from a live seed. What no reading of the disk settles is which edges the doc's 'four hops' counts ‚Äî hence the park. Whatever the ruling: the cite is re-retrieved, re-dated, and re-worded to state the hop-counting it encodes, never left asserting a bare number its source does not carry. The two narration cites deferring to it ‚Äî 384-385 ('as the format documents') and 483-484 ('the `at-import` recursion depth the format documents') ‚Äî carry the same claim and move with it, as does the propagation comment at 508-511 ('capped at the format's import recursion depth').

`the_import_closure_is_hop_capped` (1238) already pins the boundary mechanically rather than in a comment ‚Äî `r1..r5` rescued from a live seed, `r6` dead ‚Äî so it encodes today's 5 in the one place a flip cannot silently miss, and it is the test that moves if the ruling is four. Its comment (1241-1243) spells 'the format's five-hop import recursion' and carries the same claim as the constant's cite; re-word it to the ruled reading rather than leaving the fixture narrating the retired number.

tests.asserts: the import closure rescues a chain exactly as deep as the ruled cap and no deeper ‚Äî the boundary hop pinned at the ruled number, in the ruled counting

acceptance: `MAX_IMPORT_HOPS`, its doc cite, and the two narration cites that defer to it all agree with the live docs, and each states which hop-counting it encodes (spec-system.md, 'Form rules': an external fact carries its source and retrieval date where it is enforced, verified against current docs, never encoded from memory). The boundary hop stays pinned by a test at the ruled number. `cargo test` green.
notes: Park re-tested at 0c25b2c and holds ‚Äî nothing ruled the hop semantics, and neither cited file has moved since 4a33911, so every line cite above still resolves (`MAX_IMPORT_HOPS` reads 5 at graph.rs:62 under a cite claiming five). The entry stays parked: the constant may still be wrong, the one thing that keeps it out of the staleness-rider class. rescoped at 4a33911.

summary: The packaging remainder after the linux-x64+win32-x64 first cut: darwin binaries (Apple notarizing), the plugin/bundle + marketplace.json, and the binary‚Üînpm version lockstep at the v0.1 tag

gate.reason: Human release actions: John's Apple notarizing for the darwin standalone binary + darwin optionalDependency package; the v0.1 launch tag that flips the binary's self-reported crate version (0.1.0) into lockstep with npm's 0.0.x. Channel 3 (plugin/bundle + marketplace.json) is a git repo + marketplace.json + free community web form (verified 2026-07-06, code.claude.com/docs) ‚Äî no signing creds ‚Äî but rides the same release-workflow build-out. release.yml (channel 2, linux-x64+win32-x64) + the SDK launcher/optionalDependencies + standalone tag assets shipped 07-11; the standalone-asset step is wired but unexercised until a tag fires.

edit.description: Extend the existing release.yml (linux-x64 + win32-x64 first cut, the darwin deferral stated at lines 7-9) with the darwin matrix targets (aarch64/x86_64-apple-darwin) + an Apple notarization step, and a channel-3 step producing/publishing the `temper bundle` output with its marketplace.json. Distinct from the standalone tag-asset step, already wired (unexercised until a tag).

acceptance: A stranger acquires temper on all three OSes without a Rust toolchain (darwin binary notarized + pinned), or via the plugin/bundle (marketplace.json); the binary self-reports 0.1.0 in lockstep with the v0.1 npm cut (distribution.md: prebuilt binaries install on all three OSes; lockstep engine pin).
notes: Release infra outside src/; cargo gates can't validate npm/bundle publish. Park re-tested on disk at 0c25b2c and holds on every clause: `git tag -l` carries only the four era tags and no version tag, crate 0.1.0 vs npm 0.0.7, and release.yml:7-9 still states the darwin + channel-3 deferral verbatim. scoped at b676656.
